title: Productive Use Of Energy For Agricultural Value Chains In Rural Areas
summary: Build a verified workflow for mapping the gap between electricity access and productive use in agricultural value chains at sub-national scale in LMICs, with explicit mini-grid customer data limitations, agricultural value-chain specificity, and the distinction between electricity availability and verified productive use.
why_it_matters: Electrification alone does not generate rural development — productive use in income-generating activities drives prosperity. World Bank estimates 20-30 percent income gains from productive agricultural electrification, but fewer than 10 percent of SSA mini-grids serve productive-use customers. Mini-grid operators track connections, not productive use. Without verified productive-use data, energy-access investments cannot be targeted where they would most increase rural incomes.

success_metrics.target: Every productive-use claim documents whether data comes from mini-grid operator surveys, enterprise surveys, or agricultural value-chain studies, and explicitly states coverage limitations.
success_metrics.validation_method: Schema validation and reviewer spot-checks against productive-use data documentation.

success_metrics.target: No income-impact claim is merged without documenting that electricity access does not equal verified productive use — equipment adoption, affordability, technical capacity, and market access are all necessary conditions.
success_metrics.validation_method: Reviewer confirms access-vs-productive-use distinction in every effectiveness claim.

success_metrics.target: Every productive-use analysis documents which value chains (milling, drying, cold storage, irrigation, processing) are analyzed and their specific electricity-demand requirements.
success_metrics.validation_method: Red-team reviewer confirms value-chain specificity and electricity-demand documentation.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, productive-use safety review, and replication for quantitative outputs.
